1_1_1.requirement: Supplier Name / Factory Name — Cover page, Inspection Details — Both fields must be filled in English using the combined format: `Supplier Name (Factory Name)`. Neither field may contain Chinese characters or show only one of the two names.

then.question: Is the product name a real product name (not a SKU/barcode string)?

1_1_2.requirement: Product Name — Cover page / POs & product details, Name (Main identifier) — The product name field must show the real product name, not the SKU or barcode string. QIMAone auto-populates the SKU by default; the inspector must replace it manually. Check both the cover page and the POs & product details section.

then.question: Does this field satisfy the GI requirement stated for this checkpoint?

1_1_3.requirement: PO Number — Cover page, Inspection Details — If no PO was found on site, the field must read exactly "N/A" with no additional comment. The field must not be left blank.

then.question: If MFG/EXP/Batch are all N/A, is that combination explicitly justified in remarks?

1_1_4.requirement: MFG Date / EXP Date / Batch Code — Cover page, Inspection Details — If all three date/batch fields are "N/A", they must be replaced with the sentence: "Refer to the individual batch/date code in the date code check section". If at least one field has a real value, no replacement is needed.

1_1_5.requirement: Location — Cover page, Inspection Details — The location field must be written entirely in English. No Chinese characters are permitted.

then.question: Is the quantity unit a real unit (Pcs/Boxes/Packs/…) and not 'Other'?

1_1_6.requirement: Unit — Cover page / POs & product details — The unit field must not display "Other". If "Other" appears, it must be replaced with the actual unit found in the booking — typically "Boxes" or "Packs".

1_2_1.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The overall result must match the Pass/Pending/Fail/Missing Inspection logic defined in Section 8. Verify that the result shown is consistent with all findings in the body of the report.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

1_2_2.requirement: Inspector's Remark — Cover page — Every Pending or Failed report must contain both of the following remarks: (1) "The inspection result is [Pending/Failed] due to [reason]." (2) "All defective items (and failure findings) are presented to the factory." Both must be present. Neither is optional.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

1_2_3.requirement: Inspector's Remark — Cover page — The remark must be in English. Chinese characters in inspector-written remark fields are not acceptable. Exception: date codes recorded exactly as printed on the product may appear in Chinese.

2_1_1.requirement: Attachments section — end of report — The Measurement Table Excel file must be attached. Verify its presence. Also verify that the filename does not contain Chinese characters — a Chinese filename prevents the report team from opening the file.

2_2_1.requirement: Green Seal Sample (GSS) — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — The GSS result must be "Yes" (available on site and valid) for any report with an overall result of PASS. If GSS is absent, expired, or invalid, the report must be PENDING. Verify the GSS result is consistent with the overall result.

2_2_2.requirement: Any remark, photo caption, or checklist field throughout the report — The term "GSS" is the only accepted terminology for the approval sample. "Approved Sample", "Golden Sample", or any equivalent must never appear anywhere in the report text or photo captions.

2_2_3.requirement: Re-Sealed Green Seal Sample — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — After using the GSS for comparison, the inspector must re-seal it in a polybag with a signed QIMA sticker showing the date and order number, and upload a photo. Verify the re-sealed GSS photo is present in the Product Specifications section.

3_1_1.requirement: Carton Shipping Mark — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — Photos of the shipping mark must be present for both the front face and the side face of the carton. If an inner shipping mark exists, it must also be photographed.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

3_1_2.requirement: Carton Check: Dimensions and Weight — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — For each product style, the report must record: printed size (L×W×H in mm), actual measured size, printed weight (kg), and actual measured weight. If actual values differ from printed spec, a remark is required. A difference alone does not trigger Pending or Fail.

3_2_1.requirement: Individual Pack AQL table — Workmanship section — An Individual Pack AQL table must be present if and only if the product has individual packaging. If the product has no individual pack, the table must be absent. Verify against the product description and photos.

4_1_1.requirement: Workmanship AQL tables — Workmanship section (Consumer Pack, Individual Pack, Destructive Sample) — Three separate AQL tables must be present: Consumer Pack, Individual Pack (if applicable), and Destructive Sample. Each must have its own result. Verify sampling levels match the standard: Consumer Pack = Level II (onsite) or Level I (remote); Individual Pack = S4 (restorable pack) or S3 (destructive/sticker); Destructive Sample = S1.

4_1_2.requirement: Sample size and Max (acceptance number) — Workmanship AQL tables — The "Checked" number must match the sample size in the DFI Sampling Plan. The "Max" acceptance numbers must match: sample size 8 → 0/0/0; sample size 13 → 0/0/1; sample size 32 → 0/1/3. Destructive sample size is 8 if lot ≤ 35,000 and 13 if lot > 35,000.

4_1_3.requirement: AQL values (CRI/MAJ/MIN) — Workmanship AQL tables — AQL values for general products must be Not Allowed / 1.5 / 4.0. Earplug products: same (Not Allowed / 1.5 / 4.0). Protection Mask products: percentage-based values must match the DFI Sampling Plan attachment exactly.

4_1_4.requirement: Colors, Labels, Artworks, Accessories and Markings result — Workmanship section — If no special finding was observed in this category, the result must be "OK". QIMAone sometimes auto-generates "N/A" — this must be manually corrected to "OK" if there is no finding.

4_2_1.requirement: Defect list and comments — Workmanship section — If no defects were found, the text "No Defects Found" must appear in the defect section. If defects were found, each defect must have at least one supporting photo with a QIMA sticker or arrow clearly indicating the defect.

then.question: Does this field satisfy the GI requirement stated for this checkpoint?

4_2_2.requirement: Defect comments and Important Remark — Workmanship section — If multiple defects were found on the same sample, only the most serious is counted in the AQL. A remark must appear in both the Defect Comment and the Important Remark following this template: "Found [X] pcs with multiple defects: [most serious] (counted into AQL) + [other] (excluded from AQL), see defect pictures below, all defects are arrowed."

4_2_3.requirement: AQL table and remark — Workmanship section — If a defect was found outside the workmanship check stage (e.g. during quantity check or dimension measurement), it must NOT appear in the AQL workmanship table. Instead, a remark must describe it separately, the report must be set to Pending, and the full consumer pack must be sealed in a polybag with a QIMA sticker.

5_1_1.requirement: Date Code Check table — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — Date codes must be recorded exactly as printed: same format, separators, and casing. The format AND printing position must match between GSS and actual product. The actual values (dates, lot numbers) may differ — this is acceptable.

then.question: If MFG/EXP/Batch are all N/A, is that combination explicitly justified in remarks?

5_1_2.requirement: Date Code Check table — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — At least one of the three codes must be present: MFG Date / EXP Date / Lot# (Batch#). If a code is absent on the product, record "N/A" in that field. If no date code AND no batch code is found on any packaging: this is a back-office escalation situation — verify that a remark and a Pending result are present.

5_1_3.requirement: Date Code Check table — multiple batches or sub-products — If the product has multiple batches or multiple sub-products (e.g. a travel set), all date codes must be clearly separated by batch and item keywords, with 1 batch per line. Chinese date codes must be recorded in Chinese exactly as printed.

5_1_4.requirement: Barcode Details — DFI(H&B) Date code check & Barcode details checklist — The full barcode number must be recorded for each packaging level: Individual Pack / Consumer Pack / Outer Carton. If no Individual Pack exists, that field must show "N/A" — it must not be left blank.

5_2_1.requirement: Measurement table — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Only specs explicitly claimed on the product name, label, or packaging must be measured. Do not measure specs not claimed anywhere. Both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack claimed specs must be measured. The Measurement Table is a template — only rows relevant to the actual product should be filled.

5_2_2.requirement: Measurement results — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Apply pass/fail logic per spec type: (1) Volume, Quantity, Weight (general product): actual ≥ label claim → PASS; actual < label claim (even by 0.01) → FAIL. (2) Weight (health product — identified by NIP panel) and Size/Dimension: tolerance required; within tolerance → PASS; outside tolerance in either direction → FAIL.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

5_2_3.requirement: Measurement remark — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — For any tolerance-based check (dimension or health product weight), the tolerance range must be stated in the remark regardless of pass or fail outcome. Required format: "Checked OK, the tolerance range of [weight/dimension] is XXX – XXX."

5_2_4.requirement: Measurement table values — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — If all 8 (or 13) samples show exactly the same value for a measurement, flag as a likely data entry error. Real measurement variation across samples is expected — identical values across all samples are suspicious.

then.question: Does this field evidence satisfy: For Protection Mask products, the individual mask net weight must always be measured and recorded, even if no weight spe?

5_2_5.requirement: Measurement table — Protection Mask products — For Protection Mask products, the individual mask net weight must always be measured and recorded, even if no weight spec is claimed on the label.

then.question: Does this field evidence satisfy: For health products (identified by a Nutrition Information Panel on the label), both total net weight and individual ser?

5_2_6.requirement: Measurement table — Health products (products with NIP panel) — For health products (identified by a Nutrition Information Panel on the label), both total net weight and individual serving weight must be measured if the serving size per piece is stated on the NIP. Examples: "1 Capsule/1.043g" → weigh 1 capsule; "3 Tablets/5.4g" → weigh 3 tablets; "1370mg/softgel" → weigh 1 softgel.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

5_3_1.requirement: Carton drop test result — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — The carton drop test result must follow this logic: PASS if no damage on product (carton dented but product OK = PASS); PENDING if carton broken/opened but product undamaged; FAIL if product is damaged or falls out. Any outcome other than a fully clean pass requires a descriptive remark.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

5_3_2.requirement: Carton drop test result — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — The carton drop test is mandatory for all products. Exceptions: fragile products (ceramics, glass — factory documentation must be checked), and product "Guardian WEIGHING SCALE BR9012" from supplier "Camry Electronic". If the factory refused: result must be PENDING with a remark.

5_4_1.requirement: Adhesive test result — test checklist (e.g. DCL 013 - Dairy Farm - Toiletry V9 H&B) — An adhesive test failure (printing unreadable after tape removal) must result in overall report = PENDING, never FAIL. Verify consistency between adhesive test result and overall result.

items.question: Quote the tape model stated in this comment, or null

5_4_2.requirement: Adhesive test remark — test checklist — The tape model used must be stated in the remark. Accepted models: 3M 500, 3M 600P, 3M 810.

then.question: Does the bound remark/comment satisfy the GI requirement?

5_4_3.requirement: Adhesive test remark — test checklist (exemption products) — For products on the adhesive test date code exemption list (cotton tips in canisters from SOFT TIP (THAILAND) CO., LTD and WISE EVER ENTERPRISES LIMITED; PE bag products including Gurdian/Mannings amino acid bubble cleansing towel and Mannings Face Towel 10s), the date code area is exempt. If this exemption applies, a mandatory remark must be added: "Adhesive test on the date code waived due to limitation of the can and ink." All other printed areas on these products must still be tested.

5_5_1.requirement: Rubbing test result — test checklist — The rubbing test must be performed on all printed areas and markings on both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present). The test must be performed with both dry cloth (10 strokes, 9N force) and wet cloth. Result = PASS if printing clear and cloth unstained; FAIL if printing rubbed off, dimmed, or illegible.

then.question: Does this field evidence satisfy: The cover photo must show the Consumer Pack (retail packaging), frontal angle, vertical orientation, with the full produ?

6_1_1.requirement: Cover photo — Cover page — The cover photo must show the Consumer Pack (retail packaging), frontal angle, vertical orientation, with the full product name clearly readable.

6_1_2.requirement: GSS photos — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — A photo of the GSS showing the DFI QA Team label with signature must be present. The re-sealed GSS photo (polybag + signed QIMA sticker) must also be present.

6_1_3.requirement: GSS comparison photos — DFI(H&B) Product Specifications checklist — GSS vs actual product comparison photos must cover: all four faces (top, bottom, front, back) for Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present); date code area close-up; composition/ingredient label; volume or quantity claims; all barcodes and markings; product inside (capsules, liquid, etc.). Each element must appear in a photo.

6_1_4.requirement: Measurement photos — DFI(H&B) Product Dimensions & Weights checklist — Every spec that was measured must have at least one photo per sample (minimum 8 photos for a sample size of 8). Weight photos must show the scale display readable with the product on the scale. Dimension photos must show the measuring tool and product.

6_1_5.requirement: Carton drop test photos — DFI(H&B) Shipping Carton Verification checklist — Three photo stages are required: (1) before drop showing intact carton; (2) during drop; (3) after drop showing carton exterior condition AND interior product condition. Interior product photo is especially critical when any damage or deformation is observed.

6_1_6.requirement: Adhesive and Rubbing test photos — test checklist — Adhesive test photos must clearly show the tape being applied or removed on the date code area specifically, for both Consumer Pack and Individual Pack (if present). Rubbing test photos must show the cloth on the product surface.

6_1_7.requirement: Factory Review photos — QIMA Factory Review checklist — The following photos must be present: factory entrance/gate (confirming location), inspection room lighting, weighing scale and measuring tools with calibration certificates visible, and all three signed QIMA documents (Factory Disclaimer, Draft Report, COC).

then.question: Does this field evidence satisfy: No photo in the report may show a worker's face, name badge, or ID in a way that identifies the individual. If visible, ?

6_2_1.requirement: Any photo throughout the report — No photo in the report may show a worker's face, name badge, or ID in a way that identifies the individual. If visible, this is a data privacy issue.

7_1_1.requirement: AQL tables — Workmanship section — Verify the AQL levels and acceptance numbers match the product type and inspection mode (onsite vs remote):

8_1_1.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The overall result must follow this logic exactly.

8_1_2.requirement: Overall Inspection Result — Cover page — The adhesive test is the only test where failure triggers PENDING rather than FAIL. All other test failures (rubbing test, function test, sensory check, barcode scanning, back adhesive test for pantyliners, bristle pull test) trigger FAIL. Verify the overall result matches this distinction.
